title: Fix skill loading

tasks.title: Add command-skill taxonomy matrix asset tests
tasks.verification: pnpm exec vitest run src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts passed (5 tests). Earlier red evidence showed missing Load site taxonomy and missing skill-ref inventory entries before docs/ref updates.
tasks.implementation_summary: Created plugin/src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts with structural inventory for literal .opencode/command skill(...) calls and load-site classifications. Added ADV_INSTRUCTIONS load-site taxonomy docs. Replaced command-level skill("prioritizer") and skill("global-verify") refs with embedded protocol wording to eliminate phantom command skill refs. Verified with pnpm exec vitest run src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts (5 passed).
tasks.notes: Added skill-loading policy asset tests with RED then GREEN evidence. The test covers load-site taxonomy, command skill-ref inventory, shipped-skill resolution, fallback/degradation text, and worker explicit skill-denial guard. Updated taxonomy docs and removed phantom command skill refs needed to satisfy the new guard.

error_recovery.last_error: Initial test run failed as expected: ADV_INSTRUCTIONS missing 'Load site' taxonomy and inventory missing adv-opportunity-scout, prioritizer, global-verify command refs. Subsequent run exposed ADV_INSTRUCTIONS line ceiling by +1 and one unrelated concurrent-signaling integration timeout from accidentally broad pnpm test invocation; fixed line ceiling and used targeted vitest invocation.
error_recovery.next_strategy: Proceed to ready tasks; use targeted vitest for asset tests and reserve full suite for verification task.

attempts.error: pnpm test -- src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts failed: missing Load site and missing inventory entries.
attempts.diagnosis: RED evidence for newly-added asset tests; docs and command ref cleanup not implemented yet.
attempts.fix_tried: Added load-site taxonomy docs; inventoried adv-opportunity-scout; removed phantom command refs for prioritizer and global-verify.

attempts.error: Broad pnpm test invocation failed: ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md 951 lines > 950 and unrelated concurrent-signaling integration timeout.
attempts.diagnosis: Line ceiling was in-scope prose-load regression; integration timeout came from broad test invocation, not targeted asset test.
attempts.fix_tried: Compressed ADV_INSTRUCTIONS taxonomy wording and reran targeted vitest command.

tasks.title: Document load-site taxonomy and classify command-skill refs
tasks.verification: pnpm exec vitest run src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts passed (58 tests). Documentation/classification changes were included in prior checkpoint; worktree is clean.
tasks.implementation_summary: Documented orchestrator-only, worker-only, split, and inlined-agent-methodology load sites. Classified shipped command skill refs including scout split-loading, adv-tron inlined-agent-methodology, shared slop detection, and orchestrator-only skills. Verification passed via pnpm exec vitest run src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts (58 tests).
tasks.notes: Load-site taxonomy and command-skill classification are documented in ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md and covered by asset tests.

tasks.title: Add phantom-skill detection and resolve missing refs
tasks.verification: pnpm exec vitest run src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts passed (5 tests). grep found no command refs to skill("prioritizer") or skill("global-verify"). Worktree clean.
tasks.implementation_summary: The new skill-loading policy asset test extracts literal skill(...) calls across .opencode/command/*.md, ignores documented dynamic placeholders, and fails if any literal command skill is absent from shipped repo skills. The task resolution removed skill("prioritizer") from adv-discover.md in favor of the inline Tradeoff Prioritizer Protocol and removed skill("global-verify") from ship.md in favor of the embedded Global Verification Contract. Verification: targeted skill-loading test passed; grep found no command refs to skill("prioritizer") or skill("global-verify").
tasks.notes: Phantom command skill refs are detected structurally and the missing prioritizer/global-verify command refs were removed from command surfaces.

tasks.title: Update scout split-loading contracts and tests
tasks.verification: pnpm exec vitest run src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts src/adv-autonomy-quality-assets.test.ts passed (36 tests).
tasks.implementation_summary: Updated adv-discover and adv-design scout phases so the orchestrator owns ScoutCandidate schema, routing, fallback/degradation, adoption, and mutations while prompting adv-researcher to load adv-opportunity-scout in worker context when available. Updated adv-opportunity-scout skill docs to document the split-load pattern. Updated adv-discover and advance-workflow specs to encode the split-load contract and worker skill-load degradation. Added asset-test assertions for split-load command/spec/skill wording. Verification: pnpm exec vitest run src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts src/adv-autonomy-quality-assets.test.ts passed (36 tests).
tasks.notes: Scout contracts now use split-load language across commands, specs, skill docs, and asset tests.

tasks.title: Add worker skill-load availability and fallback guards
tasks.verification: pnpm exec vitest run src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts passed (6 tests). grep found no explicit skill:false in agent frontmatter and command fallback/degradation wording is present for worker skill-load.
tasks.implementation_summary: Worker self-load availability is guarded by structural tests that reject explicit skill:false on worker targets instead of requiring skill:true. Scout split-load command/spec wording now defines worker skill-load unavailable as an INCONCLUSIVE degradation path. Verification: pnpm exec vitest run src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts passed (6 tests); grep found no explicit skill:false in agent frontmatter and fallback/degradation text in relevant command surfaces.
tasks.notes: Worker skill-load guard is covered by asset tests and command/spec fallback wording.

Formatting fix for the new asset test was committed in this verification checkpoint.
tasks.notes: Contract and repo verification completed. The only validation warning is expected NO_DELTAS because this change updates tracked spec assets directly rather than registering ADV deltas.

attempts.diagnosis: New/updated test file needed standard formatting after added split-load assertions.
attempts.fix_tried: Ran pnpm exec prettier --write src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts and reran pnpm run check.

wisdom.content: For targeted Vitest evidence in plugin/, prefer `pnpm exec vitest run <file>` over `pnpm test -- <file>` when exact file selection matters; the latter can still run broader suites and surface unrelated integration timeouts.

For command skill-ref asset tests, fallback/degradation assertions should be scoped near each skill reference, not file-wide, so additional refs in the same command cannot accidentally inherit unrelated fallback wording.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved proposal inline with Tier A reply: continue.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery findings persisted; acceptance criteria approved by user reply `approve`; agreement persisted and ChangeContract minted.

design.approval_evidence: Design persisted; design leverage scout executed; independent validator returned CAUTION; caution findings incorporated; no unresolved conflict, user-value tradeoff, or contract-compromise risk.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved prep plan with reply `approve`; task graph contains 6 tasks with contract refs and validation passed with warning only (NO_DELTAS, expected before implementation).

execution.approval_evidence: All 6 implementation/verification tasks done. Verification evidence: targeted asset tests passed (89 tests); strict ADV validation passed with NO_DELTAS warning only; pnpm run check passed; pnpm test passed; pnpm run build passed.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User accepted delivered work with Tier A reply `accept`. Review verdict APPROVED; contract review matrix has 28 rows with 0 failing; executive summary persisted and verified.

items.text: Define a command/skill loading taxonomy that distinguishes orchestrator-only, worker-only, split, and inlined-agent-methodology patterns.
Inventory and classify every command `skill(...)` reference, including dynamic skill discovery and missing/external skill references.
Preserve orchestrator-owned authority for ADV state, gates, user checkpoints, adoption/routing, and mutation.
Move or route worker-only methodology to worker context only when skill-tool availability and fallback behavior are machine-checkable.
Add asset/spec tests for taxonomy compliance, phantom skill references, fallback/degradation requirements, and worker skill-availability assumptions.
Preserve existing opportunity-scout semantics while reducing main-context load for worker-only prompt content.
`ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` documents taxonomy: main-only, worker-only, split, and `inlined-agent-methodology` when optimal.
All `.opencode/command/*.md` `skill(...)` references are inventoried and classified.
Stale or missing skill refs are removed if unused; otherwise they are fixed or explicitly allowlisted with rationale.
Worker self-load is allowed only when tests verify no explicit deny and fallback behavior exists.
Orchestrator retains state, gate, user-checkpoint, adoption/routing, and mutation authority.
Existing scout semantics remain: ≤5 candidates, evidence required, strict schema, narrow auto-adopt only, and INCONCLUSIVE degradation.
Asset/spec tests fail on taxonomy violations, phantom skill refs, missing fallback, or unsafe worker-load assumptions.
Do not change the seven-gate lifecycle.
Do not replace or rename existing ADV sub-agents.
Do not weaken structured output or report requirements.
Do not remove fallback or degradation paths for skill-backed commands.
Do not allow nested sub-agent delegation.
Keep skills read-only guidance; skills do not own ADV mutations.
Do not let sub-agents own ADV gate completion, state mutation, or user checkpoint routing.
Do not auto-adopt sub-agent recommendations without orchestrator-owned routing rules.
Do not rely on prose-only policy without asset/spec tests where machine checks are possible.
Do not leave stale or missing skill references unclassified.
Do not preserve duplicate skill-vs-agent-prompt methodology without a classification or drift-control decision.
Replacing or renaming existing ADV sub-agents.
Changing the seven-gate lifecycle.
Global prompt optimization unrelated to command/skill loading boundaries.
Runtime token accounting or context-window measurement.

rows.evidence: ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md documents load-site taxonomy; skill-loading asset test asserts taxonomy values.
plugin/src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts inventories literal .opencode/command/*.md skill(...) refs and compares scanned refs to SKILL_REF_INVENTORY.
rows.notes: Dynamic placeholders excluded by explicit DYNAMIC_SKILL_REFS.

rows.evidence: adv-discover.md/adv-design.md split-load wording keeps schema, routing, fallback/degradation, adoption, and ADV mutations with orchestrator.
skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts checks worker targets have no explicit skill:false and commands include fallback/degradation; scout commands define worker skill-load unavailable as inconclusive.
pnpm exec vitest run src/skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts src/adv-autonomy-quality-assets.test.ts src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts passed (89 tests).
rows.notes: Covers taxonomy, phantom refs, fallback locality, worker no-deny, scout split-load.

rows.evidence: adv-opportunity-scout skill and scout command/spec wording preserve ≤5 candidates, evidence, strict schema, narrow auto-adopt, and INCONCLUSIVE degradation.
skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts asserts ADV_INSTRUCTIONS contains Load site plus all four taxonomy values.
skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts scans command files and requires scanned literal refs equal SKILL_REF_INVENTORY.
skill-loading-policy-assets.test.ts verifies literal command skill refs resolve to shipped repo skills; grep found no command refs to skill("prioritizer") or skill("global-verify").
rows.notes: global-verify removed from ship command; prioritizer command ref replaced with embedded protocol wording.

rows.evidence: worker load-site asset test rejects explicit skill:false for worker agents and fallback/degradation tests pass; scout commands include worker skill-load unavailable degradation.
rows.notes: No explicit worker skill deny found.

rows.evidence: Review confirmed no skill/worker wording owns ADV gates, state mutation, user checkpoints, or adoption routing.
adv-autonomy-quality-assets.test.ts and skill-loading tests pass; skill docs retain ≤5, evidence, ScoutCandidate schema, narrow auto-adopt, INCONCLUSIVE.
New asset tests fail on missing taxonomy values, inventory drift, phantom shipped-skill mismatch, missing nearby fallback, explicit worker skill denial, and missing scout split-load wording.
rows.notes: Reviewer strengthened fallback locality per skill ref.

rows.evidence: No gate lifecycle code or command sequence changed; only docs/spec/test/skill command wording changed.
No sub-agent files renamed/replaced; scout continues to use adv-researcher and adv-tron remains existing agent.
ScoutCandidate 8-field schema and REVIEWER_REPORT expectations preserved; tests assert schema wording remains.
Fallback/degradation tests pass; reviewer strengthened fallback checks to be near each skill ref.
No command or skill adds nested sub-agent delegation; scout remains one adv-researcher spawn under orchestrator.
Skills remain read-only guidance; adv-opportunity-scout explicitly says commands own mutations.
Split-load wording explicitly keeps gates/state/checkpoints/adoption/mutations with orchestrator.
Scout routing still auto-adopts only narrow low-risk contract-tied candidates; all other candidates surface to user.
Structural asset tests enforce taxonomy, phantom refs, fallback/degradation, worker-deny, and scout split-load.
Inventory plus shipped-skill test fails stale/missing literal command skill refs; prioritizer/global-verify command refs removed.
adv-tron classified as inlined-agent-methodology; scout classified split; embedded methodology rows documented.
Not in scope; no sub-agent replacement/rename performed.
Not in scope; seven-gate lifecycle unchanged.
Not in scope; changes limited to command/skill boundaries and tests.
Not in scope; no runtime token accounting added.

acceptanceCriteria: `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` documents taxonomy: main-only, worker-only, split, and `inlined-agent-methodology` when optimal.
All `.opencode/command/*.md` `skill(...)` references are inventoried and classified.
Stale or missing skill refs are removed if unused; otherwise they are fixed or explicitly allowlisted with rationale.
Worker self-load is allowed only when tests verify no explicit deny and fallback behavior exists.
Orchestrator retains state, gate, user-checkpoint, adoption/routing, and mutation authority.
Existing scout semantics remain: ≤5 candidates, evidence required, strict schema, narrow auto-adopt only, and INCONCLUSIVE degradation.
Asset/spec tests fail on taxonomy violations, phantom skill refs, missing fallback, or unsafe worker-load assumptions.
